jokes.text: Well, then you leave, Terry. And pay for the cake you already ate.
jokes.setup: Terry can't choose between wedding cakes because they're all so good
jokes.punchline: Leslie's blunt dismissal combined with demanding payment for consumed cake
jokes.explanation: Pure Leslie Knope - her intensity about wedding planning combined with petty enforcement of rules. Well-structured with clear setup/payoff.

jokes.text: That was a joke cake I got from the grocery store, to weed out low-quality palates.
jokes.setup: Someone likes the vanilla cake with lemon cream
jokes.punchline: Leslie reveals it was a deliberate test cake from the grocery store
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character moment showing Leslie's obsessive perfectionism and slight elitism. The phrase 'low-quality palates' is perfectly Leslie.

jokes.text: Maybe it's because he's my fiancé and I love him, but I kind of like the grocery store one, too.
jokes.setup: Leslie just revealed the grocery store cake was a test for 'low-quality palates'
jokes.punchline: Donna admits she also likes it, undermining Leslie's test
jokes.explanation: Sweet callback that shows Donna's love making her biased, while also deflating Leslie's pretentious cake test.

jokes.text: That's why I'm about to give her a surprise bigger than when LeBron went back to Miami.
jokes.setup: Tom is being the Butler of Honor and taking the job seriously
jokes.punchline: The absurd comparison to LeBron James' career moves for a wedding surprise
jokes.explanation: Tom's tendency to make pop culture references, though the LeBron reference feels slightly dated and the joke is just okay.

jokes.text: Treat Yo' Self. Treat Yo' Self 2017!
jokes.setup: The reveal of DJ Bluntz and mysterious cake
jokes.punchline: The iconic 'Treat Yo' Self' catchphrase returns
jokes.explanation: Iconic running gag that fans love. High impact due to nostalgia and quotability, though not particularly original at this point.

jokes.text: I still don't know what's happening. / So, what's happening? / Treat Yo' Self! / Not an answer to my question.
jokes.setup: Terry is genuinely confused about the Treat Yo' Self situation
jokes.punchline: Tom keeps responding 'Treat Yo' Self' to direct questions, Terry calls it out
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of confusion with Terry as the straight man pointing out the absurdity of Tom's non-answers.

jokes.text: But then, we checked thousands of emails and texts between our customers and their loved ones, and it seemed like people thought we were wrong.
jokes.setup: CEO says they didn't think they invaded privacy
jokes.punchline: The casual admission that they read thousands of private communications to figure this out
jokes.explanation: Sharp satire of tech company tone-deafness. The casual admission of massive privacy violation to determine if they violated privacy is perfectly absurd.

jokes.text: seating is gonna be organized by income and sexual history.
jokes.setup: Gryzzl announces free U2/Beyoncé concert to win back trust
jokes.punchline: The seating arrangement reveals continued invasive data collection
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation showing they haven't learned anything. 'Sexual history' is an inspired absurd detail that makes it funnier.

jokes.text: And that, I swear, is the last time that we will be peeking inside your computers.
jokes.setup: The previous violations and the seating by sexual history
jokes.punchline: The promise to stop while describing current invasive practices
jokes.explanation: Good follow-up that emphasizes the company's complete lack of self-awareness about privacy invasion.

jokes.text: All we need is a blimp and a decoy blimp.
jokes.setup: Leslie wants something big and exciting to counter Gryzzl
jokes.punchline: Andy's solution involves two blimps, one of which is a decoy
jokes.explanation: Perfect Andy Dwyer logic - the 'decoy blimp' detail pushes it into absurdist territory. Short, memorable, and very character-specific.

jokes.text: Okay. For what I hope is the last time, but we'll assume is not the last time, our strategy does not involve blimps.
jokes.setup: Andy suggests blimp-based solutions again
jokes.punchline: Leslie's weary but knowing response about Andy's blimp obsession
jokes.explanation: Good character callback with Leslie's resignation to Andy's predictable suggestions. The phrasing shows their long relationship.

jokes.text: I am not a spy, and I would never reveal classified information. / Do you have to say that every time? / Yes.
jokes.setup: Ron makes his standard disclaimer
jokes.punchline: Leslie's exasperation and Ron's matter-of-fact response
jokes.explanation: Solid running gag showing Ron's bureaucratic rigidity and Leslie's familiarity with his quirks.

jokes.text: And just like Joey Fatone and Lance Bass, we are totally in sync.
jokes.setup: Leslie talking about her and Ron teaming up
jokes.punchline: The NSYNC pun using the two least famous members
jokes.explanation: Decent pun that's very Leslie - overly specific pop culture reference. Using the lesser-known NSYNC members adds to the humor.

jokes.text: We're in sync emotionally. Stop patting my head.
jokes.setup: Leslie makes the NSYNC pun
jokes.punchline: Ron apparently starts literally patting her head, which she has to stop
jokes.explanation: Great physical comedy beat showing Ron's awkward way of showing affection and Leslie having to manage it.

jokes.text: Let me repeat that because it makes me very horny. C'mon, Knope. $125 million.
jokes.setup: The Newport Trust announces selling to Gryzzl for $125 million
jokes.punchline: The inappropriate sexual response to the money amount
jokes.explanation: Appropriately gross character moment that emphasizes greed. The direct address to Leslie makes it more personal and awkward.

jokes.text: Do mine first. In case something happens to you, I wanna make sure I get paid. Looking out for number one. Always smart.
jokes.setup: April asks Ron to sign checks
jokes.punchline: Her matter-of-fact explanation about his potential death and Ron's approval
jokes.explanation: Perfect April-Ron dynamic. Her dark pragmatism paired with his approval of her selfish honesty is classic character comedy.

jokes.text: Well, breakups are famously super fun and make everyone feel awesome. So, it's no different.
jokes.setup: April asks how post-breakup life is going
jokes.punchline: Tom's sarcastic response about breakups being fun
jokes.explanation: Standard sarcasm about breakups. Well-delivered but not particularly original or memorable.

jokes.text: I literally couldn't care less.
jokes.setup: Lucy tells Tom to text her when he decides about the wedding
jokes.punchline: Claims not to care immediately after giving detailed instructions
jokes.explanation: Good contradiction showing Lucy does care despite claiming otherwise. Standard romantic comedy beat.

jokes.text: You should come to the wedding and do paperwork.
jokes.setup: Tom awkwardly inviting April to Donna's wedding, getting more uncomfortable
jokes.punchline: The suggestion reaches peak awkwardness with doing paperwork at a wedding
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation of awkwardness. 'Do paperwork' at a wedding is beautifully absurd and captures Tom's nervous rambling perfectly.

jokes.text: Stop, you're making this sound almost too fun.
jokes.setup: Tom suggests she come do paperwork at the wedding
jokes.punchline: April's dry response to the ridiculous suggestion
jokes.explanation: Perfect April response - dry, sarcastic, and delivered with her characteristic deadpan style.

jokes.text: Today is about one thing. Things. Things, Donna! Our favorite.
jokes.setup: Tom explains what Treat Yo Self day is about
jokes.punchline: Reduces it to the abstract concept of 'things' with enthusiasm
jokes.explanation: Captures Tom and Donna's materialistic philosophy perfectly. The repetition of 'things' is funny.

jokes.text: All I wanted was 25 square miles of land valued at $100 million given to me for free. Is that too much to ask?
jokes.setup: Leslie lamenting her loss in the land battle
jokes.punchline: The completely unreasonable nature of what she considers a modest request
jokes.explanation: Great Leslie moment showing her complete lack of perspective when it comes to her ambitious projects. The specific dollar amount makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Voodoo is still an option.
jokes.setup: Leslie lamenting their loss
jokes.punchline: April's matter-of-fact suggestion of supernatural solution
jokes.explanation: Perfect April deadpan suggestion. The casual delivery of something absurd is very in character.

jokes.text: One place asked me if I wanted kale in my milkshake. My milkshake, you guys.
jokes.setup: Leslie complaining about too much kale in town
jokes.punchline: The personal violation of suggesting kale in her sacred milkshake
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor about health food trends, with Leslie's personal outrage making it character-specific.

jokes.text: Was it Putin? Voldemort Putin, of Russia? I'd love to take that bastard down.
jokes.setup: JJ mentions a weird-sounding landlord name
jokes.punchline: Leslie's immediate jump to Putin with the 'Voldemort' middle name
jokes.explanation: Leslie's tendency to see political conspiracies everywhere. 'Voldemort Putin' is a funny escalation showing her mindset.

jokes.text: Tiny rolled-up scrolls delivered by trained foxes. I'm on it.
jokes.setup: Leslie asks April to use whatever communication method young people use
jokes.punchline: April's completely absurd but deadpan answer
jokes.explanation: Excellent April moment - completely absurd response delivered with perfect deadpan. 'Trained foxes' is an inspired detail that makes it memorable and quotable.

jokes.text: Oh, hell, yeah. Blimp time.
jokes.setup: Leslie asks Andy if he's thinking what she's thinking
jokes.punchline: Andy immediately assumes it's blimp-related
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback showing Andy's one-track mind about blimps. Great character consistency.

jokes.text: That's not true. It's called 'constructing a narrative,' JJ.
jokes.setup: JJ corrects Leslie's claim that he built the diner with his own hands
jokes.punchline: Leslie admits to lying for political purposes
jokes.explanation: Great meta-joke about political spin. Leslie's casual admission of narrative construction is very her.

jokes.text: I do not like rallies. And based on what I see from this vantage point, I do not like most of you.
jokes.setup: Ron is introduced to speak at the rally to save JJ's
jokes.punchline: His immediate hostility toward the crowd he's supposed to rally
jokes.explanation: Pure Ron Swanson - antisocial, direct, and completely counter to what's expected at a rally. Perfect character integration and very quotable.

jokes.text: Please do not approach me on the street after this event and attempt to talk to me. Our similarities begin and end with this single issue.
jokes.setup: Ron's hostile rally speech about breakfast food
jokes.punchline: His preemptive rejection of any future social interaction with supporters
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation of Ron's antisocial nature. The formal language makes his rejection even funnier.

jokes.text: Occasionally on my show, we deal with real issues like bullying, peer pressure, or holding in farts.
jokes.setup: Andy explaining his serious Jonathan Karate persona
jokes.punchline: The list that escalates from serious issues to 'holding in farts'
jokes.explanation: Good Andy moment mixing genuine children's issues with juvenile humor. The escalation works well.

jokes.text: In those very special episodes, I become Jonathan Karate, the very serious older brother of Johnny Karate.
jokes.setup: Andy explains his character transformation
jokes.punchline: Creates family relationship between his personas
jokes.explanation: Good expansion of Andy's show mythology. The brother relationship adds depth to his absurd universe.

jokes.text: Well, make it quick. I gotta go pull the plug on my dad in 10 minutes.
jokes.setup: Dennis being interrupted by Jonathan Karate
jokes.punchline: The casual mention of a dark family situation
jokes.explanation: Darkly funny character moment that establishes Dennis as callous. The casual tone about such a serious situation is effectively shocking.

jokes.text: I'm gonna turn that place into an elbow art salon and make a killing.
jokes.setup: Dennis explaining his plans for JJ's Diner
jokes.punchline: The bizarre concept of an 'elbow art salon'
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully absurd business concept that fits Dennis's pretentious character. 'Elbow art salon' is specific and memorable.

jokes.text: Ladies and gentleman, off to our right, you can see the house that belongs to Bruce Willis and Christina Aguilera.
jokes.setup: Celebrity homes tour in Beverly Hills
jokes.punchline: The implication that Bruce Willis and Christina Aguilera live together
jokes.explanation: Minor joke about unlikely celebrity roommates. Functional but not particularly strong.

jokes.text: I would like three big-ass diamonds surrounded by their own set of tiny-ass diamonds.
jokes.setup: Tom at an expensive Beverly Hills salon getting elbow art
jokes.punchline: His over-the-top diamond request with the 'big-ass'/'tiny-ass' phrasing
jokes.explanation: Good Tom moment showing his materialistic excess. The phrasing is funny and character-appropriate.

jokes.text: Treat yo' self.
jokes.setup: Thomas asks Tom what he recommends
jokes.punchline: Simple callback to the episode's theme
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective callback. Gets its power from established running gag rather than originality.

jokes.text: Psych!
jokes.setup: Dennis appears to agree to let JJ's stay after the crowd reaction
jokes.punchline: The brutal reversal revealing it was all a fake-out
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection with maximum economy. One word completely reverses the emotional trajectory of the scene.

jokes.text: and you can all just sit there pulling on your puds.
jokes.setup: Dennis's villainous rant about not caring about the diner
jokes.punchline: The crude and inappropriate phrase delivered to a crowd
jokes.explanation: Crude humor that fits Dennis's character but isn't particularly clever. Gets a reaction through shock rather than wit.

jokes.text: Give me your boos! I am nourished by your hatred!
jokes.setup: Dennis reveling in being the villain
jokes.punchline: The dramatic declaration about being 'nourished by hatred'
jokes.explanation: Great villain moment with theatrical language. 'Nourished by your hatred' is a memorable, quotable phrase that fits Dennis perfectly.

jokes.text: I gotta go kill my dad.
jokes.setup: Dennis leaving the rally after his villain speech
jokes.punchline: Casual callback to pulling the plug on his father
jokes.explanation: Good callback that emphasizes Dennis's callousness. The casual phrasing makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Water makes it worse. Water makes it worse.
jokes.setup: Everyone trying to deal with the cologne smell
jokes.punchline: Desperate repetition as the solution backfires
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy escalation. The repetition shows growing panic but isn't particularly original.

jokes.text: Bogdano-delish.
jokes.setup: Tom eating Peter Bogdanovich's saltwater eel
jokes.punchline: The pun combining the director's name with 'delicious'
jokes.explanation: Typical Tom wordplay. Simple pun that fits his character but isn't particularly clever.

jokes.text: I was secretly hoping you got me the shoes Jaden Smith wears in Hitch 2: Son of a Hitch.
jokes.setup: Tom hoping for a gift from Donna
jokes.punchline: The completely made-up movie sequel with Jaden Smith
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist humor with Tom's specific pop culture obsessions. The fake movie title is cleverly constructed.

jokes.text: Josh Groban! He's eating his own sushi! That's the most baller thing I've ever seen!
jokes.setup: Celebrity sushi made from fish previously owned by celebrities
jokes.punchline: Tom's excitement about Josh Groban eating sushi made from his own fish
jokes.explanation: Funny escalation of the absurd restaurant concept. Tom's enthusiasm about this bizarre situation is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: A more accurate name for this place? Medical Waste Butt-Sweat Grove.
jokes.setup: Leslie explaining the fancy name 'Beachview Terrace' for a bad neighborhood
jokes.punchline: Her brutally honest alternative name
jokes.explanation: Leslie's tendency toward specific, gross imagery when she's frustrated. The contrast with the fancy name makes it funnier.

jokes.text: This place has everything. Vermin, bullet holes, a hornet's nest.
jokes.setup: Showing the terrible property
jokes.punchline: Describing horrible features as if they're selling points
jokes.explanation: Good ironic real estate humor. The escalation from vermin to bullet holes to hornets works well.

jokes.text: Oh, my God. Where'd you come from? / The floor hole.
jokes.setup: Andy appears suddenly during their planning
jokes.punchline: His matter-of-fact explanation about emerging from a hole in the floor
jokes.explanation: Perfect Andy moment - the casual explanation of something completely absurd. 'The floor hole' is beautifully matter-of-fact.

jokes.text: We're losing the cranberry salad, effective immediately!
jokes.setup: Craig briefly becomes Tom's replacement as boss
jokes.punchline: His immediate power-mad decision about the cranberry salad
jokes.explanation: Good Craig moment showing his intensity and immediate need to make dramatic changes when given power.

jokes.text: You've been hoaxed! You've been Tommy-ed!
jokes.setup: Tom trying to backtrack after being vulnerable
jokes.punchline: Creates fake verb form of his own name
jokes.explanation: Great Tom ego moment. Making his name a verb for pranking shows his self-involvement perfectly.

jokes.text: Are you tripping?
jokes.setup: Leslie and Ron suggesting Gryzzl move to the bad neighborhood instead
jokes.punchline: The CEO's casual use of outdated slang
jokes.explanation: Minor character humor showing the tech CEO trying to be hip. Works but isn't particularly strong.

jokes.text: We wouldn't have to hire Banksy.
jokes.setup: The buildings come 'pre-graffitied'
jokes.punchline: The casual mention of hiring famous street artist Banksy for corporate graffiti
jokes.explanation: Good satire of corporate culture appropriating street art. The casual mention of hiring Banksy is funny.

jokes.text: We wouldn't have to hire Banksy.
jokes.setup: Buildings come pre-graffitied
jokes.punchline: Tech company would pay famous artist for graffiti
jokes.explanation: Good tech company satire. The idea they'd hire Banksy for corporate graffiti is perfectly absurd.

jokes.text: Here's a far-out idea that just came to the top of my brainium.
jokes.setup: Leslie proposing the land donation
jokes.punchline: Uses made-up word 'brainium' while mimicking tech speak
jokes.explanation: Good Leslie attempt to speak tech bro language. 'Brainium' is a perfect Leslie made-up word.

jokes.text: And truthfully, they're all shrooming super hard at Coachella right now.
jokes.setup: CEO needs to consult his board and legal team
jokes.punchline: Casual admission that the corporate leadership is on psychedelic drugs at a music festival
jokes.explanation: Great tech company satire showing the casual attitude toward drugs and responsibilities. Perfectly captures Silicon Valley culture.

jokes.text: Left eye. I'm watching porn with my right eye.
jokes.setup: CEO asks to upload something to his GryzzlGlass
jokes.punchline: The TMI revelation about what he's doing with his other eye
jokes.explanation: Crude tech humor that fits the character but relies more on shock than wit. Gets a reaction but isn't particularly clever.

jokes.text: You need to tell me if Gavin is pooping. / No, do not laugh, okay? / You're not even Caleb. You're Gavin. You're Caleb.
jokes.setup: Andy realizes one of his ninjas is missing
jokes.punchline: The escalating confusion about which kid is which while asking about bathroom habits
jokes.explanation: Good Andy moment showing his childlike approach to supervising actual children. The escalating confusion is well-structured.

jokes.text: You need to tell me if Gavin is pooping.
jokes.setup: Andy realizes he's lost track of the kids
jokes.punchline: Urgent concern about bathroom status during crisis
jokes.explanation: Great escalation of Andy's panic. The specific bathroom concern shows his childlike priorities.

jokes.text: You sat on that news and let me stew here for three hours? / Yup.
jokes.setup: Ron reveals he's known about their success for hours
jokes.punchline: His unapologetic admission to letting Leslie worry unnecessarily
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron behavior - his matter-of-fact cruelty delivered with zero remorse. Classic dynamic between Leslie and Ron.

jokes.text: I do not approve of this. / Don't care.
jokes.setup: Leslie hugging Ron in celebration
jokes.punchline: Ron's disapproval and Leslie's dismissive response while continuing to hug
jokes.explanation: Classic Leslie-Ron interaction showing their relationship dynamics. Simple but effective character comedy.
